Paragraph: Prince William of Wales today attended the World Scout Jamboree at Hylands Park, Chelmsford, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Essex (the Lord Petre).

The Princess Royal, President of the Patrons, this morning visited Crime Concern's Youth Works Project, Preston Road Regeneration Centre, Flinton Grove, Kingston upon Hull, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of the East Riding of Yorkshire (Mr. Richard Marriott). Her Royal Highness, President of the Patrons, Crime Concern, later attended a seminar at BP Amoco Sports and Leisure Club, Saltend, Hedon, East Riding of Yorkshire. The Princess Royal, Patron, the National Autistic Society, this afternoon attended a Lunch at Sickling Hall, Sicklinghall Village, Wetherby, and was received by Mrs. Judith Thomas (Deputy Lieutenant of North Yorkshire). Her Royal Highness, Patron, the National Autistic Society, later opened the Robert Ogden School, Clayton Lane, Thurnscoe, Rotherham, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of South Yorkshire (the Earl of Scarbrough).

The Duchess of Gloucester this afternoon visited Lady Eleanor Holles School, Hanworth Road, Hampton, Middlesex, and unveiled a stone plaque to commemorate its Three Hundredth Anniversary, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Greater London (Sir David Brewer).

The Earl and Countess of Wessex this morning visited Salute the NHS, Hangar 113, Bicester Motion, Skimmingdish Lane, Bicester, Oxfordshire. His Royal Highness this evening attended a Surrey Heath Prepared Meeting via video link. The Countess of Wessex this afternoon held a Meeting with the Lord Hague of Richmond (Co-Founder, Preventing Sexual Violence in Conflict Initiative) via video link. Her Royal Highness, Patron, Brendoncare Foundation, later held a Meeting via video link with Mrs. Carole Sawyers upon relinquishing her appointment as Chief Executive Officer and Mrs. Marianne Wanstall upon assuming the appointment.

The Princess Royal, Patron, RYA Sailability, this morning visited Colne Valley Special Sailors, the Aquadrome, Rickmansworth, and was received by Miss Linda Haye (Deputy Lieutenant of Hertfordshire). Her Royal Highness this afternoon visited Building Research Establishment Certification, Bucknalls Lane, Garston, Watford, and was received by Lady Staughton (Deputy Lieutenant of Hertfordshire). The Princess Royal, Patron, Sense - the National Deafblind and Rubella Association, later re-opened buildings at the Anne Wall Centre, 12 Hyde Close, Barnet, and was received by Mr. Stephen Folkes (Deputy Lieutenant of Greater London). Her Royal Highness this evening visited the Royal Horticultural Society's Chelsea Flower Show at the Royal Hospital Chelsea, London SW3. The Princess Royal, President, International League for the Protection of Horses, was represented by Mrs. Timothy Holderness-Roddam at the Service of Thanksgiving for Mrs. Vivien McIrvine (Past Vice President) which was held in the Guards Chapel, Wellington Barracks, Birdcage Walk, London SW1, today.

Princess Alexandra this afternoon opened the Michael Letcher Pathology Complex at the Princess Alexandra Hospital NHS Trust, Hamstel Road, Harlow, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Essex (the Lord Petre).

The Duke of York this morning visited Helmsley Town Hall, North Yorkshire, to mark its centenary, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant (the Lord Crathorne). His Royal Highness, Patron, The Duke of York's Community Initiative, opened the Regen Centre, Riccall, North Yorkshire. The Duke of York this evening attended a Reception for the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association at the Palace of Holyroodhouse.

The Prince of Wales, Patron, Macmillan Cancer Care, this morning received Mr Richard Hambro, former Chairman, and Mr Jamie Dundas, his successor. His Royal Highness later attended the Memorial Service for Sir Harry Secombe which was held at Westminster Abbey. The Prince of Wales, Patron, Business in the Community, this afternoon chaired a meeting of the 'Hereford Sustain Report', a subsidiary of the recently launched initiative 'Business Action in the Rural Community'. His Royal Highness, President, The Mary Rose Trust, later received the Chairman (Sir David Cooksey). The Prince of Wales later received the Minister of State (Environment), Department of the Environment, Food and Rural Affairs (The Rt Hon Michael Meacher MP). His Royal Highness, President, The Mary Rose Trust, this evening gave a dinner.

The Duke of Kent this evening visited Glasgow and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant for the City of Glasgow (Mr. Pat Lally). His Royal Highness later attended a Gala Dinner to launch the first Schottish World Trade Exhibition & Conference, ScotWorld 1999.
The Duke of Kent this evening attended a Reception at 55 Prince's Gate, London SW7, to mark the Seventy Fifth Anniversary of Ognisko Polskie, the Polish Hearth Club.
The Princess Royal, Honorary Air Commodore, Royal Air Force Brize Norton this morning attended 101 Squadron's Centenary Parade at Royal Air Force Brize Norton, Carterton, Oxfordshire.

The Duke of York, Patron, the Centre for Engineering and Manufacturing Excellence, this morning opened the Centre for Engineering and Manufacturing Excellence Business Accelerator Launchpad Centre, Marsh Way, Rainham, Essex.

The Princess Royal, President, Animal Health Trust, this morning attended an Industry Committee Meeting at Buckingham Palace. Her Royal Highness, Patron, RYA Sailability, this afternoon was present at the launch of the Peter Harrison Foundation at the Quayside Restaurant, St. Katharine's Dock, London E1. The Princess Royal, Patron, British Quality Foundation, afterwards attended the United Kingdom Business Excellence Forum at the London Hilton, Park Lane, London W1, and this evening attended the United Kingdom Business Excellence Award Ceremony at the London Hilton.
The Princess Royal, Patron, Evelina Children's Hospital Appeal, this afternoon opened Evelina Children's Hospital, Lambeth Palace Road, London SE1.
Prince Henry of Wales, Patron, Invictus Games Foundation, this morning attended the Invictus Games at Walt Disney World, Orlando, Florida, United States of America.
The Duke of Edinburgh, Patron, Cornwall Rugby Football Union, this afternoon attended the County Championship Final at Twickenham, Middlesex.

The Queen today visited Cambridgeshire and was received upon arrival at Ely Railway Station by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant (Mr. Hugh Duberly). Her Majesty drove to Ely Cathedral and, having been received by the Bishop of Ely (the Right Reverend Dr. Anthony Russell) and the Mayor of Ely (Councillor Richard Hobbs), was joined by The Duke of Edinburgh, Patron, Order of St. Etheldreda, and attended a Service to celebrate the Nine Hundredth Anniversary of the Diocese of Ely. The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh, Chancellor, University of Cambridge, were entertained to Luncheon this afternoon at King's College, King's Parade, Cambridge. Her Majesty and His Royal Highness, Chancellor, afterwards attended a Ceremony at the Senate House, King's Parade, Cambridge, to commemorate the Eight Hundredth Anniversary of the University of Cambridge. The Queen addressed those present and Her Majesty and His Royal Highness subsequently attended a Reception. The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh later visited Marshall Group, the Airport, Newmarket Road, Cambridge, to mark its Centenary and were received by the Chairman of Marshall Group Companies (Mr. Michael Marshall). Her Majesty and His Royal Highness, escorted respectively by the Chairman and Mr. Robert Marshall (Chairman, Marshall Motor Group), toured the site, viewing displays of aircraft, a hangar and the control building, and The Queen unveiled a plaque to mark the visit. The Duke of Edinburgh, Chancellor, University of Cambridge, this morning opened the Alan Reece Building at the Institute for Manufacturing, 17 Charles Babbage Road, Cambridge.

The Duke of Gloucester, Joint President, Cancer Research UK, this afternoon attended a Concert of Christmas Carols and Readings in Glasgow Cathedral and was received by Mrs. Jean McFadden (Deputy Lieutenant of the City of Glasgow). The Duchess of Gloucester, Royal Colonel, 7th Battalion The Rifles, this evening attended The Rifles' Regimental Awards Dinner at Guildhall, London EC2.
The Princess Royal, Second Warden, the Fishmongers' Company, this morning received Mr. Nigel Bankes (Prime Warden) and Commodore Toby Williamson RN (Clerk).

The Duke of Gloucester, Colonel-in-Chief, The Royal Anglian Regiment, this morning reviewed the Gloucestershire Army Cadet Force and afterwards attended a Lunch at Royal Air Force Innsworth. His Royal Highness this afternoon attended a Service in Gloucester Cathedral to mark the Millennium of the County of Gloucestershire. The Duchess of Gloucester, Patron, Fleming Fulton School, this afternoon attended a Service in St. Anne's Cathedral, Belfast, to mark the Fiftieth Anniversary of the School, and was received by Commander Keith Cochrane RNR (Vice Lord-Lieutenant of the County Borough of Belfast).
The Duke of Gloucester this evening attended the St. George's House Annual Lecture in St. George's Chapel, Windsor Castle.
Princess Alexandra, Honorary Patron, American Associates of the Royal Academy Trust, this evening attended a Gala Dinner at the Pierre Hotel, 2 East 61st Street, New York, United States of America.
Princess Alexandra this morning opened the Charles Carter Building and attended a Reception at Lancaster University, Bailrigg, Lancaster.
The Duke of Kent, Grand Master, United Grand Lodge of England, this morning received Dr. David Staples (Grand Secretary and Chief Executive Officer) at Kensington Palace.

The Prince Charles, Duke of Rothesay and The Duchess of Rothesay this afternoon attended Beating Retreat by the Massed Pipes and Drums and the Band of the Royal Regiment of Scotland at Balmoral Castle.

The Duke of York, Captain, the Royal and Ancient Golf Club of St. Andrews, this morning visited Prestwick Golf Club, Ayrshire, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Ayrshire and Arran (Major Richard Henderson). His Royal Highness this afternoon attended the Open Championship at Royal Troon Golf Club, Troon, Ayrshire. The Duke of York this evening attended the Golf Writers Association Dinner at Royal Troon Golf Club.

The Duke of Kent, President, Commonwealth War Graves Commission, today received Mr. Alan Pateman-Jones (Director General). His Royal Highness, President, Royal United Services Institute for Defence and Security Studies, this afternoon attended a Lecture entitled "All Politics are Global" given by His Excellency Mr. Louis Susman (Ambassador of the United States of America) at Whitehall, London SW1.

The Prince of Wales, Patron, Yorkshire Agricultural Society, and The Duchess of Cornwall this morning visited the Great Yorkshire Show, Hookstone, Harrogate, and were received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of North Yorkshire (the Lord Crathorne). His Royal Highness, Patron, Royal Hall Restoration Trust, this afternoon visited the Royal Hall, Valley Drive, Harrogate. The Duchess of Cornwall this afternoon opened the Newby Hall Stables conversion in Ripon and was received by Colonel Edward York (Vice Lord-Lieutenant of North Yorkshire).

Princess Alexandra, accompanied by Mr. and Mrs. James Ogilvy and Miss Marina Ogilvy, attended the Service of Thanksgiving for the Life of the Rt. Hon. Sir Angus Ogilvy which was held in Westminster Abbey today.
The Duke of Gloucester, Patron, Nuffield Farming Scholarships Trust, this afternoon, attended a Luncheon at Savills, 33 Margaret Street, London W1.
The Queen has been pleased to appoint the Lord Luce to be a Permanent Lord in Waiting to Her Majesty.

The Prince of Wales this afternoon visited the Nigerian School of Infantry, Kaduna, Nigeria. His Royal Highness called upon The Emir of Zazzau at the Palace. The Prince of Wales later visited the Old Mud Church, Wusasa, and met members of the Anglican community. His Royal Highness subsequently visited Arewa House, Kaduna. The Prince of Wales this evening arrived at Royal Air Force Lyneham from Nigeria. Mr. Clive Alderton, Squadron Leader Jayne Casebury RAF and Mrs. Hanna Howard were in attendance. The Duchess of Cornwall this evening switched on Tetbury Christmas lights and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Gloucestershire (Mr. Henry Elwes).

The Prince of Wales, President, The Prince of Wales's Institute of Architecture, this morning chaired a meeting of the Council. His Royal Highness, Patron, this afternoon held a Reception for the Rare Breeds Survival Trust.

The Princess Royal, Patron, the Challenge Business International Limited, accompanied by Rear Admiral Timothy Laurence, this evening attended the Global Challenge Ball at the London Hilton, Park Lane, London W1.

The Duke of Kent today visited Merseyside and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant (Mr. Alan Waterworth). His Royal Highness this morning visited Rainhill City Learning Centre, St. Helens. The Duke of Kent this afternoon attended a presentation about the City's European Capital of Culture at Liverpool Town Hall. His Royal Highness later visited Speke Hall, the Walk, Liverpool.
Prince Henry of Wales, Patron, Invictus Games Foundation, today attended the Invictus Games Toronto 2017 in Ontario, Canada.

The Duke of Kent, Colonel-in-Chief, The Royal Regiment of Fusiliers, today received Lieutenant Colonel Jeremy Dodd upon relinquishing his appointment as Commanding Officer and Lieutenant Colonel Steven Burton upon assuming the appointment.

The Prince of Wales today carried out the following engagements in Oman: His Royal Highness this morning visited the Association for Early Intervention to help children with special needs in Muscat and presented a cheque raised by the Embassy during Oman-Britain Friendship Week. The Prince of Wales later met His Highness Sayyid Haithem bin Tariq Al Said, Minister for National Heritage and Culture; His Highness Sayyid As'Ad bin Tariq Al Said, Representative of His Majesty the Sultan of Oman, and His Highness Sayyid Fahad bin Mahmoud Al Said, Deputy Prime Minister for Cabinet Affairs. His Royal Highness this afternoon visited the Sultan Qaboos Grand Mosque, and later met with Shaikha Aisha al Siabia, Head of Public Authority for Craft Industries and Mr. Taha Sulaiman al Kishry, Director, Oman Fine Arts Society, and viewed work of artists. The Prince of Wales this evening attended a Dinner given by His Majesty Sultan Qaboos bin Said.

The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall, on behalf of The Queen in celebration of Her Majesty's Diamond Jubilee, today carried out the following engagements in New South Wales, Australia. Their Royal Highnesses this morning visited the Royal Australian Navy Heritage Centre, Pyrmont. The Prince of Wales, Patron, the Campaign for Wool, later attended a celebration of the Australian wool industry at the Museum of Contemporary Art, 140 George Street, Sydney. His Royal Highness this afternoon met members of New South Wales' First Response community at Icebergs Club, Bondi Beach, 1 Notts Avenue, Bondi, and was received by the Hon. Barry O'Farrell MP (Premier of New South Wales). The Prince of Wales afterwards met participants of the National Rugby League's programme, "Dream Believe Achieve", at Bondi Beach Surf Lifesaving Club, Queen Elizabeth Drive. His Royal Highness later received practitioners of sustainable agriculture. The Prince of Wales subsequently received representatives of The Prince's Charities Australia. Their Royal Highnesses this evening attended a Diamond Jubilee Reception at Sydney Opera House, 2 Macquarie Street, Sydney, given by the Governor of New South Wales (Professor Marie Bashir) and Sir Nicholas Shehadie. The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall afterwards attended a Dinner at Admiralty House, Kirribilli, given by the Governor-General of Australia and Mr. Michael Bryce. The Duchess of Cornwall, Colonel-in-Chief, The Royal Australian Corps of Military Police, this morning visited Victoria Barracks, Sydney. Her Royal Highness this afternoon attended a Reception at Government House, Sydney, given by the Governor of New South Wales for leading women in the community.

The Prince of Wales, Patron, the Battle of Britain Fighter Association, was represented by Air Chief Marshal Sir Richard Johns at the annual Service of Thanksgiving and Rededication for the Battle of Britain which was held at Westminster Abbey, London SW1, today.
The Earl of Wessex, Trustee, The Duke of Edinburgh's Award International Foundation, today attended the Trustees' Working Committee Meeting at Award House, 7-11 St. Matthew Street, London SW1.
The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall this afternoon gave a Reception for Police at Sandringham House.
The Duke of Kent, Patron, the Hanover Band, this evening attended a Concert in Chichester Cathedral, West Sussex.
Princess Alexandra, President, Sightsavers, this morning departed from Heathrow Airport, London, for the United States of America. Mrs. Diane Duke is in attendance.

The Duke of Gloucester, President, British Consultants and Construction Bureau, today chaired the panel of judges for the British International Expertise Awards 2004 at 1 Westminster Palace Gardens, Artillery Row, London SW1. His Royal Highness afterwards attended the Natural Resources International Photographic Exhibition at the Department for International Development, 1 Palace Street, London SW1. The Duchess of Gloucester, Honorary President, the Lawn Tennis Association, this evening attended a Reception at the National Tennis Centre, Bank of England Sports Ground, Bank Lane, Roehampton, London SW15.

The Duke of Gloucester, Grand Prior, Order of St John, this morning opened the Westbury Centre, Fairfield Road, Framlingham and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Suffolk (the Lord Tollemache). His Royal Highness afterwards opened the Robert Orme Centre at Warden's Centre for Disabled People, Ness House, Sizewell, Leiston, Suffolk. The Duchess of Gloucester, Honorary President, the Lawn Tennis Association, this afternoon visited the site of the new National Tennis Centre, Bank of England Sports Ground, Bank Lane, Roehampton, London SW15.
The Duke of Sussex, Joint Patron, the Royal Foundation of The Duke and Duchess of Cambridge and The Duke and Duchess of Sussex, this evening attended a Reception at Buckingham Palace.

The Princess Royal today carried out the following engagements in Bahrain. Her Royal Highness this morning called upon the Prime Minister of the Kingdom of Bahrain (His Highness Shaikh Khalifa bin Sulman Al Khalifa) at Government House, Manama. The Princess Royal afterwards received the Chairman of the Supreme Council for Women (Her Highness Shaikha Sabeeka bint Ibrahim bin Mohammad Al Khalifa) at Al Rowda Palace, Rifa'a. Her Royal Highness later visited the National Stadium, Isa Town. The Princess Royal this afternoon visited St. Christopher's Senior School, Isa Town. Her Royal Highness was received by The King of the Kingdom of Bahrain at Al Safriya Palace. The Princess Royal afterwards visited The King's Stables at Al Safriya Palace. Her Royal Highness this evening attended a Reception given by the Director of the British Council (Mrs. Sandra Hamrouni) at her Residence, Adliya. The Princess Royal later attended a Dinner given by Her Majesty's Ambassador to the Kingdom of Bahrain (His Excellency Mr. James Bowden) at the Residence, Manama.

The Earl and Countess of Wessex this morning visited Otter Brewery, Mathayes, Luppitt, Honiton, and were received by Sir John Cave, Bt. (Vice Lord-Lieutenant of Devon). Their Royal Highnesses later visited Quickes Traditional Limited, Home Farm, Newton St. Cyres, Exeter, and were received by Mr. Timothy Key (Deputy Lieutenant of Devon). The Earl of Wessex this afternoon attended a Lunch with the Council of Reserve Forces' and Cadets' Associations Reservists and Local Employers at Wyvern Barracks, Exeter. The Earl and Countess of Wessex later visited Powderham Church, Devon Historic Churches Trust, Powderham, Exeter. Their Royal Highnesses subsequently attended a Reception for Devon Historic Churches Trust at Powderham Castle. The Countess of Wessex this afternoon visited Whetman Pinks Nursery Limited, Houndspool, Ashcombe, Dawlish, and was received by Lady Clifford (Deputy Lieutenant of Devon).

The Duchess of Gloucester, Patron, the Bobath Centre for Children with Cerebral Palsy and Bobath Cymru, this morning visited Bobath Scotland, 2028 Great Western Road, Glasgow. Her Royal Highness, Patron, Scottish Opera, this afternoon attended a performance of Die Walküre at the Edinburgh Festival Theatre, Nicolson Street, Edinburgh.

The Princess Royal, Patron, the London Youth Trust, this morning attended a Consensus Conference at the Lawn Tennis Association, National Tennis Centre, 100 Priory Lane, Roehampton, London SW15. Her Royal Highness, President, Animal Health Trust, this afternoon attended a Reception at BGC International, 1 Churchill Place, Canary Wharf, London E14.

The Duke of York, Special Representative for International Trade and Investment, this afternoon received Mr. Stuart Popham (Chairman, City UK Executive) and Mr. John Ingamells (Director, City UK Executive). His Royal Highness, Special Representative for International Trade and Investment, afterwards received the Rt. Hon. Anthony Blair.

The Duchess of Gloucester, Honorary President, the Lawn Tennis Association, this morning opened Letchworth Tennis Club, Muddy Lane, Letchworth Garden City, Hertfordshire and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant (Mr Simon Bowes Lyon).

The Duke and Duchess of Cambridge today attended the Seventy Fifth Anniversary celebrations of the Special Air Service Regiment at Stirling Lines, Credenhill, and were received by Mr. Christopher James (Deputy Lieutenant of Herefordshire).
The Queen arrived at Buckingham Palace this afternoon. The Duke of Edinburgh, Patron and Life Member, Game and Wildlife Conservation Trust, this evening held a Dinner at Sandringham House.
Queen Elizabeth The Queen Mother this afternoon visited Tower Hamlets Memorial Garden, Isle of Dogs. The Lady Angela Oswald and Sir Alastair Aird were in attendance.

The President of the Republic of Turkey, accompanied by Mrs. Gül, this morning delivered an Address to members of Wilton Park (an executive agency of the Foreign and Commonwealth Office) at the Royal Society, Carlton House Terrace, London SW1, and was received by Professor Martyn Poliakoff (Foreign Secretary Elect, the Royal Society) and the Chairman of Wilton Park (Mr. Ian Ferguson). The President and Mrs. Gül later visited the Olympic Park, Stratford, London E15, and were received by the Rt. Hon. William Hague MP (Secretary of State, Foreign and Commonwealth Office), Mr. Craig Reddie (London Organising Committee for the Olympic Games) and Mr. John Armitt (Chairman, Olympic Delivery Authority). The President of the Republic of Turkey this afternoon attended a Forum Lunch at the Foreign and Commonwealth Office, King Charles Street, London SW1, and was received by the Rt. Hon. David Lidington MP (Minister of State, Foreign and Commonwealth Office) and Mr. Ali Babacan (Minister of State and Deputy Prime Minister of Turkey). The President and Mrs. Gül later called upon The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall at Clarence House. The President of the Republic of Turkey and Mrs. Gül afterwards visited the Palace of Westminster and were received by the Lord Speaker (the Baroness D'Souza) and the Speaker of the House of Commons (the Rt. Hon. John Bercow MP). The Speaker welcomed His Excellency and Mrs. Gül and The President delivered an Address. The President of the Republic of Turkey and Mrs. Gül subsequently attended a Reception with All-Party Parliamentary Groups, Peers, Members of Parliament and other guests. The President and Mrs. Gül this evening received an Address of Welcome and were entertained at a Banquet by the Rt. Hon. the Lord Mayor and the City of London Corporation at Guildhall. Prince and Princess Michael of Kent were present. The President of the Republic of Turkey and Mrs. Gül subsequently attended a Meeting with members of the Turkish Press at the Mandarin Oriental Hotel, 66 Knightsbridge, London SW1. The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh today visited Admiralty House, Parliament Street, Whitehall, London SW1, for His Royal Highness's Inauguration as Lord High Admiral and were received by the First Sea Lord and Chief of the Naval Staff (Admiral Sir Mark Stanhope). The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh met members of the Navy Board, before His Royal Highness, accompanied by Her Majesty, received the Letters Patent and the Lord High Admiral's Verge and Flag and The Duke of Edinburgh was subsequently received in the Courtyard with a Royal Salute. Her Majesty and His Royal Highness were entertained to Luncheon afterwards by the First Sea Lord and Chief of the Naval Staff. The Rt. Hon. David Cameron MP (Prime Minister and First Lord of the Treasury) had an audience of The Queen this evening. The Duke of Edinburgh this morning received the Lord Waldegrave of North Hill (Chairman, Royal Mint Advisory Committee) at Buckingham Palace. His Royal Highness, Admiral and Member, Royal Yacht Squadron, this evening attended the Winter Dinner at Butchers' Hall, 87 Bartholomew Close, London EC1.

The King and Queen of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan this afternoon visited The Prince of Wales at Clarence House. His Royal Highness this evening received the Rt. Hon. Desmond Browne MP (Secretary of State for Defence). The Princes of Wales afterwards gave a Dinner for leading chefs and restaurateurs from across Europe showcasing British beef to encourage British exports.

The Duke of York today visited South Yorkshire and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant (the Earl of Scarbrough). His Royal Highness, Patron, this morning presented The Duke of York's Community Initiative Community Awards at Sheffield Hallam University, Sheffield. The Duke of York, Special Representative for International Investment and Trade, afterwards visited Swann Morton, Sheffield. His Royal Highness, Special Representative for International Investment and Trade, this afternoon visited AES Engineering Limited, Rotherham. The Duke of York, Special Representative for International Investment and Trade, later visited Doncaster Chamber of Commerce, Doncaster Innovation Centre, Doncaster.

The Prince of Wales this morning visited the Western Isles where he was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of the Western Isles (The Viscount Dunrossil). His Royal Highness this morning visited the Taigh Chearsabhaigh Arts Centre, Lochmaddy, North Uist, Western Isles. His Royal Highness later visited Lochmaddy Surgery, North Uist. The Prince of Wales this afternoon opened the Berneray Causeway linking North Uist to Berneray. His Royal Highness joined members of the the Berneray Community for a reception at the Community Centre and presented the 'Lord of the Isles Trophy' to the winning team of the Shinty Mini Festival. His Royal Highness later visited Bernera where he was received by the Deputy Lord-Lieutenant of the Western Isles (Mr Simon Fraser). The Prince of Wales finally visited the Iron-Age Settlement at Bernera, Isle of Lewis.

The Princess Royal, Patron, National Institute of Adult Continuing Education, this morning visited Aigburth Care Home, 21 Manor Road, Oadby, Leicester, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Leicestershire (Jennifer, Lady Gretton). Her Royal Highness, Patron, National Institute of Adult Continuing Education, later attended a Meeting at Renaissance House, 20 Princess Road West, Leicester. The Princess Royal, Patron, College of Occupational Therapists, this afternoon visited St. Andrew's Healthcare, Billing Road, Northampton, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Northamptonshire (the Lady Juliet Townsend). Her Royal Highness, Royal Patron, the Wise Campaign, later opened the new Science and Technology Newton Building at the University of Northampton, Avenue Campus, St. George's Avenue, Northampton. The Princess Royal, Patron, Voluntary Services Overseas, this evening attended a Reception at Tallow Chandlers Hall, 4 Dowgate Hill, London EC4. Her Royal Highness, President, British Olympic Association, later held a Team 2012 Appeal Dinner at Buckingham Palace.

The Duke of Gloucester, Colonel-in-Chief, The Royal Anglian Regiment, this morning visited 2nd Battalion at Alexander Barracks, Dhekelia, Cyprus. His Royal Highness, Colonel-in-Chief, The Royal Anglian Regiment, this afternoon visited 3rd Battalion at the Ledra Palace Hotel, Nicosia, and subsequently attended a Dinner.

The Princess Royal, President, Animal Health Trust, this afternoon held a Fellowship Reception at Buckingham Palace. Her Royal Highness, Patron, Sense - the National Deafblind and Rubella Association, later attended the annual Supporters' Reception at Wragge and Company, 3 Waterhouse Square, 142 High Holborn, London EC1. The Princess Royal, Master, the Worshipful Company of Butchers, accompanied by Vice Admiral Timothy Laurence, this evening attended the annual City Food Lecture at Guildhall, London EC2.

The Prince Charles, Duke of Rothesay, Lord High Commissioner to the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland, this morning attended the Opening of the General Assembly at the Assembly Hall, was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of the City of Edinburgh (Mr. Eric Milligan, the Rt. Hon. the Lord Provost) and gave an Address to the Assembly. His Royal Highness afterwards visited Simpson House, a drug counselling service run by the Church of Scotland's Board of Social Responsibility. The Prince Charles, Duke of Rothesay, Patron, Dance Base, later visited the new studio premises under construction in Edinburgh. His Royal Highness this afternoon held a Garden Party in the garden of the Palace of Holyroodhouse for Members of the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland and for charitable organisations throughout Scotland. The Prince Charles, Duke of Rothesay this evening gave a Dinner at the Palace of Holyroodhouse for past and present Moderators and Lord High Commissioners of the General Assembly.
Princess Alexandra, Chancellor, this afternoon presided at Ceremonies for the conferment of Bachelor and Honorary Degrees at Lancaster University.

The Duke of Sussex this morning visited Nottingham Academy, Ransom Drive, Mapperley, Nottingham, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Nottinghamshire (Sir John Peace). His Royal Highness this afternoon visited the Community Recording Studio, Lowdham Street, St. Ann's, Nottingham.

The Duke of Kent, President, Royal National Lifeboat Institution, this morning visited North Berwick Lifeboat Station, 1 Victoria Road, North Berwick, and was received by Mr. Patrick Gammell (Vice Lord-Lieutenant of East Lothian).

The Earl of Wessex, Trustee, The Duke of Edinburgh's Award International Foundation, accompanied by The Countess of Wessex, this morning attended a Gold Award Ceremony at Beloeil Castle, Belgium, followed by a Lunch. Their Royal Highnesses this afternoon arrived at Farnborough Airport from Belgium.
The Duke of Kent, President, Commonwealth War Graves Commission, this morning presented the President's Award at St. James's Palace.
The Duke of Kent this afternoon arrived at Gatwick Airport, London, from Cyprus. Captain Alexander Potter was in attendance.

The Duke of Gloucester this afternoon attended a Reception and Concert at St. Anne's Church, Newell Street, Limehouse, London E14. The Duchess of Gloucester this afternoon opened the Mountfitchet Romeera Leisure Centre, Forest Hall Road, Stansted, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Essex (the Lord Petre). Her Royal Highness later opened the Great Dunmow Leisure Centre, Parsonage Downs, Dunmow, Essex.
The Earl and Countess of Wessex, Founders, Wessex Youth Trust, this afternoon held a Luncheon at Ascot Racecourse, Berkshire.
Divine Service was held in the Church of St. Peter and St. Paul, West Newton, this morning. The Queen presented prizes for children's Church attendance.

Princess Alexandra, Royal Honorary Freeman, the Worshipful Company of Barbers, this afternoon received Sir Barry Jackson (Master) and Mr. John Bach (Deputy Master) at Buckingham Palace. Her Royal Highness, President, afterwards presented the Royal Humane Society Silver Medal for bravery whilst saving life to Marine Darren Swift.

The Earl of Wessex this morning departed from Heathrow Airport, London, for the United States of America and was received this afternoon upon arrival at Dulles International Airport, Washington DC, by Her Majesty's Ambassador to the United States of America (His Excellency Sir Nigel Sheinwald). His Royal Highness later attended a Meeting with Mr. Arne Duncan (Secretary of State for Education) at 400 Maryland Avenue, South West Washington DC. The Earl of Wessex this evening attended a Reception at Capitol Hill. Mrs. Rosemary Hamer is in attendance.

The Prince of Wales this afternoon "turned up the ball" at the annual Royal Shrovetide Football match, Ashbourne, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Derbyshire (Mr. John Bather). His Royal Highness, President, The Prince's Trust, this evening gave a Dinner for the Trust.
The Duke of Kent, Chancellor, today opened the Anthony Kelly Wing, Surrey Technology Centre, Surrey Research Park, University of Surrey, Guildford.
The Duke of Gloucester, Vice President, Lepra, this morning held a Meeting with Mr. Geoffrey Prescott (Chief Executive) via telephone.

The Queen was represented by Dr. Douglas Glass (Apothecary to the Household at Balmoral) at the Memorial Service for Dr. Gordon Lindsay (formerly Apothecary to the Household at Balmoral) which was held in Aboyne-Dinnet Church, Aboyne, Aberdeenshire, this afternoon.

The Duke of Gloucester today visited East Sussex and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant (Mrs. Andrew Stewart-Roberts). His Royal Highness this morning opened the Information Communication Technology Suite at Stone Cross School, Adur Drive, Stone Cross, Pevensey. The Duke of Gloucester afterwards visited St. Michael's Hospice, 25 Upper Maze Hill, St. Leonards-on-Sea, to mark its Twenty First Anniversary. His Royal Highness, Patron, this afternoon visited Pestalozzi International Village Trust, Sedlescombe. The Duke of Gloucester later opened Rye Medical Centre, Kiln Drive, Rye Foreign, Rye.

The Earl of Wessex this morning departed Heathrow Airport, London, for France. His Royal Highness this evening attended a Reception given by British Ski and Snowboard at the Hotel Allodis, Meribel. The Countess of Wessex this evening attended a Scene and Heard Production of "Twist and Turn - the Giddy Plays" at Teatro Technics, 26 Crowndale Road, London NW1, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Greater London (Sir David Brewer).

The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall this morning visited Gloucestershire Royal Hospital, Great Western Road, Gloucester, and were received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Gloucestershire (Mr. Edward Gillespie). The President of the Republic of Hungary and Mrs. Göncz arrived at Windsor Castle today on a State Visit to The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh. Prince and Princess Michael of Kent welcomed The President and Mrs. Göncz on behalf of Her Majesty at Heathrow Airport and travelled by car to Windsor Home Park (Public) where His Excellency and Mrs. Göncz were met by The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh. The President of the Republic of Hungary and Mrs. Göncz, acompanied by The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh, with Prince and Princess Michael of Kent, drove in a Carriage Procession to Windsor Castle with a Sovereign's Escort of the Household Cavalry. Gun Salutes were fired in the Home Park (Public) by The King's Troop Royal Horse Artillery and from the Tower of London by the Honourable Artillery Company. Guards of Honour were provided at Windsor Home Park (Public) by Nijmegen Company, Grenadier Guards and at Windsor Castle by the 1st Battalion Welsh Guards, who later marched past. The King's Troop Royal Horse Artillery marched past and the Sovereign's Escort of the Household Cavalry ranked past. The Queen invested The President of the Republic of Hungary with the Insignia of an Honorary Knight Grand Cross of the Most Honourable Order of the Bath. The President of the Republic of Hungary, accompanied by The Duke of York, this afternoon viewed military bridge building by a joint British/Hungarian team in the Great Park, Windsor, and was received by Brigadier Albert Whitley (Engineer in Chief) and Mr. Philip Everett (Deputy Ranger, Windsor Great Park). The President afterwards visited Runnymede, was received by the Senior Trustee of the Magna Carta Trust (Sir Peter Marshall) and the Mayor of Runnymede (Councillor Peter Poole) and viewed the Magna Carta Memorial. The President of the Republic of Hungary and Mrs. Göncz subsequently visited Queen Elizabeth The Queen Mother at Royal Lodge. Mrs. Göncz this afternoon visited Savill Gardens. The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh gave a State Banquet this evening in honour of The President of the Republic of Hungary and Mrs. Göncz at which Queen Elizabeth The Queen Mother, The Prince of Wales, The Duke of York, The Princess Royal, accompanied by Commodore Timothy Laurence RN, The Duke and Duchess of Gloucester, Prince and Princess Michael of Kent, and Princess Alexandra, the Hon Lady Ogilvy, accompanied by the Rt. Hon. Sir Angus Ogilvy, were present. The following had the honour of being invited: Suite of The President of the Republic of Hungary and Mrs. Göncz: Mr. István Stumpf (Minister for the Prime Minister's Office), Mr. József Szájer (Chairman of European Integration Select Committee), Mr. Károly Szunyogh (State Secretary, Head of the Office of The President), Mr. János Herman (State Secretary, Ministry of Foreign Affairs), His Excellency the Ambassador of the Republic of Hungary and Mrs. Szentiványi, Lieutenant General Lajos Urbán (Deputy Chief of Defence Staff), Mr. András Gulyás (Chief Foreign Policy Adviser, Office of The President), Mr. András Faragó (Spokesman, Office of The President) and Mr. Lajos Illich (Chief of Protocol). Specially Attached in attendance on The President of the Republic of Hungary and Mrs. Göncz: Mrs. Christian Adams (Lady in Waiting), the Viscount Brookeborough (Lord in Waiting) and the Viscountess Brookeborough, Mr. Nigel Thorpe (Her Majesty's Ambassador to the Republic of Hungary) and Mrs. Thorpe, and Squadron Leader Simon Brailsford RAF (Equerry in Waiting). Diplomatic Corps: His Excellency the Ambassador of the Federal Republic of Germany and Frau von Moltke, His Excellency the Ambassador of the People's Republic of China and Madame Chen Xiaodong, His Excellency the Ambassador of Romania and Mrs. Onofrei, His Excellency the Ambassador of the Slovak Republic and Mrs. Slobodnikova, and His Excellency the Ambassador of the Republic of Slovenia and Mrs. Setinc. The Cabinet: The Lord Chancellor and the Lady Irvine of Lairg, the Prime Minister and Mrs. Blair, the Secretary of State for Foreign and Commonwealth Affairs and Mrs. Cook, the Secretary of State for Defence and Mrs. Robertson, and the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food (the Rt. Hon. Nick Brown MP). Special Invitations: The Archbishop of Canterbury and Mrs. Carey, the Rt. Hon. the Speaker, the Earl Marshal and the Duchess of Norfolk, the Viscount and Viscountess Rothermere, the Lord Wallace of Saltaire and Professor Helen Wallace, the Lord Chief Justice and the Lady Bingham of Cornhill, the Rt. Hon. Paddy Ashdown MP and Mrs. Ashdown, the Rt. Hon. William Hague MP and Mrs. Hague, the Rt. Hon. David Trimble MP and Mrs. Trimble, the Rt. Hon. the Lord Mayor and the Lady Mayoress, the Rt. Hon. Edward and Mrs. George, General Sir Charles and Lady Guthrie, Air Chief Marshal Sir Peter and Lady Squire, Sir Richard and Lady Wilson, Sir John and Lady Kerr, Sir John and Lady Birch, Sir John and Lady Harvey-Jones, Lady Solti, Mr. and Mrs. Stephen Cox, Professor and Mrs. Alan Watson, Mr. Bernard Matthews, Mr. and Mrs. Alexander de Gelsey, Professor and Mrs. George Radda, Major General Evelyn and the Hon. Mrs. Webb-Carter, Dr. and Mrs. Daniel Abondolo, Mr. Neal Ascherson and Ms. Isabel Hilton, Mr. and Mrs. David Challen, Mr. and Mrs. Robert Davies, Professor and Mrs. Robert Evans, Mr. and Mrs. Luke Fitzherbert, Mr. and Mrs. Gyorgy Gomori, Mr. and Mrs. Hatar Gyozo, Mr. Paul Hampton, Mr. and Mrs. John Hoskisson, Mr. and Mrs. Hugh Johnson, Mr. and Mrs. Anatol Lieven, Miss Catherine Mackintosh and Mr. Charles Peel, the Reverend Robert and Mrs. Patkai, Mr. and Mrs. Mark Rylance, Mr. and Mrs. Bernard Schreier, Dr. Michael Stewart and Dr. Cornelia Sorabji, Dr. and Mrs. Andrew Taussig, Mr. and Mrs. Edmund Wallis, Mr. and Mrs. Philip Wroughton, and Mr. and Mrs. Mel Young. The Queen held a Council at 4.30 p.m.. There were present: the Rt. Hon. Margaret Beckett MP (President), the Rt. Hon. Alan Milburn MP (Chief Secretary to the Treasury) and the Rt. Hon. Tessa Jowell MP (Minister of State, Department of Health). Mr. Edward George and Sir Alan Haselhurst MP were sworn in as members of Her Majesty's Most Honourable Privy Council. Mr. Alex Galloway was in attendance as Clerk of the Council. The Rt. Hon. Margaret Beckett MP had an audience of Her Majesty before the Council. The Duke and Duchess of Gloucester welcomed The President and Mrs. Mbeki on behalf of Her Majesty at Royal Air Force Northolt and travelled by car to the Royal Pavilion in Windsor Home Park (Public) where His Excellency and Mrs. Mbeki were met by The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh. Gun Salutes were fired in the Home Park (Public) by The King's Troop Royal Horse Artillery and from the Tower of London by the Honourable Artillery Company. Guards of Honour were provided at Windsor Home Park (Public) by the 1st Battalion Grenadier Guards and at Windsor Castle by the 1st Battalion Scots Guards, who later marched past. The King's Troop Royal Horse Artillery marched past and the Sovereign's Escort of the Household Cavalry ranked past. Her Majesty's Body Guard of the Honourable Corps of Gentlemen at Arms, The Queen's Body Guard of the Yeomen of the Guard and the Military Knights of Windsor were on duty. The Queen, accompanied by The Duke of Edinburgh, went in State to the Palace of Westminster today to open the Session of Parliament. Her Majesty and His Royal Highness drove in a Carriage Procession, escorted by a Sovereign's Escort of The Household Cavalry, under the command of Major Valentine Woyka, The Blues and Royals (Royal Horse Guards and 1st Dragoons), and were received at the Sovereign's Entrance by the Lord Great Chamberlain and the Deputy Earl Marshal. Guards of Honour were mounted at Buckingham Palace by The Queen's Guard found by Nijmegen Company Grenadier Guards, under the command of Major Guy Denison-Smith, and at the Palace of Westminster by the 1st Battalion Grenadier Guards, under the command of Major John Wrench. A staircase party of The Household Cavalry was on duty at Victoria Tower, House of Lords. Royal Salutes were fired in Green Park by The King's Troop Royal Horse Artillery, under the command of Major William Connell, and from the Tower of London Saluting Battery by the Honourable Artillery Company, under the command of Captain William Sallitt. The Imperial State Crown, the Sword of State and the Cap of Maintenance were conveyed previously to the House of Lords in a Carriage Procession, escorted by a Regalia Escort of The Household Cavalry. Her Majesty's Body Guard of the Honourable Corps of Gentlemen at Arms was on duty in the Prince's Chamber and The Queen's Body Guard of the Yeomen of the Guard was on duty in the Royal Gallery. The Ladies and Gentlemen of the Household and the Pages of Honour to The Queen (Lord Maltravers, the Hon. John Bowes-Lyon, Viscount Garnock and Lord Carnegie) were in attendance at the Palace of Westminster. The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh returned to Buckingham Palace and were received by the Lord Chamberlain and the Vice-Chamberlain of the Household. Her Majesty and His Royal Highness attended Ascot Races this afternoon.

Rosie Winterton MP (Minister of State, Department of Health) and the Lord Davies of Oldham (Captain of The Queen's Body Guard of the Yeomen of the Guard). Her Royal Highness, President, this evening attended the Gypsy Moth Royal Gala Dinner and Australian Yachting Awards at Doltone House Restaurant, Jones Bay Wharf, Pyrmont Point, New South Wales, and was received by the Governor of New South Wales (Professor Marie Bashire).

The Duke of Gloucester, Grand Prior, Order of St. John, this afternoon received the Governor-General of Saint Lucia (Her Excellency Dame Pearlette Louisy) and invested Her Excellency as a Dame of Grace of the Most Venerable Order of the Hospital of St. John of Jerusalem.

The Princess Royal this morning visited the Russian Therapeutic Riding Club, Hippodrome, Moscow. Her Royal Highness afterwards visited the Action for Russian Children sponsored project "Centre for Curative Pedagogics", Moscow. The Princess Royal this afternoon attended a Lunch at the Swiss Diamond Hotel, Moscow, given by Mrs. Valentina Ivanovna Matvienko (Deputy Prime Minister). Her Royal Highness later called upon The President of the Russian Federation and toured the Kremlin Palace. The Princess Royal afterwards visited the "No to Alcoholism and Narcotics" Children's Shelter "the Way Home", Moscow. Her Royal Highness this evening attended a Reception for Embassy staff given by Her Majesty's Ambassador to the Russian Federation (His Excellency Sir Roderic Lyne) at the British Embassy. The Princess Royal later attended a Dinner given by Her Majesty's Ambassador to the Russian Federation at the Residence for members of the local and British communities.

The Duke of Cambridge, Commodore-in-Chief, Submarines, today attended a Service to recognise Fifty Years of Continuous At Sea Deterrence, Westminster Abbey, London SW1, and subsequently attended a Reception at the Queen Elizabeth II Centre, Broad Sanctuary, London SW1, and was received by Commander Johnathan Herriman (Deputy Lieutenant of Greater London).

The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh, with The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall, this morning arrived at Cardiff Central Railway Station and were received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of South Glamorgan (Captain Norman Lloyd-Edwards RNR), the Rt. Hon. the Lord Mayor of Cardiff (Councillor Gillian Bird) and the Chief Constable, South Wales Police (Mrs. Barbara Wilding). The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh, with The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall, drove to the National Assembly for Wales Senedd in Cardiff Bay and were received by the Presiding Officer (the Lord Elis-Thomas) and the First Minister (the Rt. Hon. Rhodri Morgan AM). Her Majesty was received with a Gun Salute fired from HMS EXETER. The Presiding Officer welcomed The Queen and Her Majesty addressed the Members of the National Assembly for Wales and the First Minister replied. The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh, with The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall, today attended a Reception at National Museum Cardiff, Cathays Park, Cardiff, to mark the Centenary of National Museum Wales. The First Minister was received in audience by Her Majesty at National Museum Cardiff. The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh, with The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall, were entertained to Lunch by the Presiding Officer this afternoon at National Museum Cardiff. The Duke of Edinburgh, Senior Fellow, this evening attended the 2007 Awards Dinner of the Royal Academy of Engineering at Christ Church Spitalfields, Commercial Street, London E1. The Lord St. John of Bletso (Lord in Waiting) was present at Heathrow Airport, London, this morning upon the Arrival of The President of the Republic of Namibia and Mrs. Pohamba and welcomed His Excellency and Mrs. Pohamba on behalf of The Queen.
The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall this evening gave a Dinner for The Prince of Wales Foundation.
The Princess Royal, President, British Olympic Association, today attended the Winter Olympic Games in Turin, Italy. Her Royal Highness this evening arrived at Gatwick Airport, London, from Italy.
Mrs. Robert de Pass has succeeded the Hon. Mary Morrison as Lady in Waiting to The Queen.

The Duke of York, Special Representative for International Trade and Investment, this morning attended a Reception for The Queen's Awards for Enterprise at the Gladstone Library, One Whitehall Place, London SW1. His Royal Highness, Special Representative for International Trade and Investment, this afternoon gave a Briefing Lunch on South Africa at Buckingham Palace. The Duke of York, Special Representative for International Trade and Investment, later received Mr. Nicholas Robinson (Head of Policy, United Kingdom Trade and Investment) upon relinquishing his appointment. His Royal Highness received Mr. Mukhtar Hussain (Global Head - Principal Investments, HSBC Bank). The Duke of York, President, this evening attended the Royal Aero Club Awards Ceremony Reception at the Royal Air Force Club, Piccadilly, London W1.
The Princess Royal, Patron, the Basic Skills Agency, this afternoon attended the End of Year Reception at Commonwealth House, 1-19 Oxford Street, London WC1.

His Excellency Dr. Singha B. Basnyat was received in audience by The Queen and presented the Letters of Recall of his predecessor and his own Letters of Credence as Ambassador from the Kingdom of Nepal to the Court of St. James's. Princess Jotshana Basnyat was also received by Her Majesty. Mr. Philip Astley (Director, Protocol, Foreign and Commonwealth Office) was present. His Excellency Mr. Karel Kühnl and Mrs. Kühnl were received in farewell audience by The Queen and took leave upon His Excellency relinquishing his appointment as Ambassador from the Czech Republic to the Court of St. James's. His Excellency Dr. Jürgen Oesterhelt and Mrs. Oesterhelt were received in farewell audience by Her Majesty and took leave upon His Excellency relinquishing his appointment as Ambassador from the Federal Republic of Germany to the Court of St. James's. Major Nigel Chamberlayne-Macdonald was received by The Queen when Her Majesty invested him with the Insignia of a Commander of the Royal Victorian Order. The President of the Republic of South Africa and Mrs. Graca Machel visited The Queen and remained to Tea. The Duke of Edinburgh, Chancellor, this morning visited the University of Cambridge and afterwards conferred Honorary Degrees at the Honorary Degrees Congregation. The Baroness Gould of Potternewton (Baroness in Waiting) Called Upon The President of the Republic of South Africa, who was accompanied by Mrs. Machel, at the Dorchester Hotel, London W1, this morning and, on behalf of The Queen, welcomed The President and Mrs. Machel on their Arrival in this Country. Mrs. Christian Adams has succeeded Mrs. Robert de Pass as Lady in Waiting to Her Majesty.
The Queen, with The Duke of Edinburgh, honoured Ascot Races with her presence today.

Princess Alexandra, President, Friends of the Elderly, this afternoon opened Bradbury Resource Centre, Lavender Road, Woking, and met users of the Centre, including members of the Day Club of Alzheimer's Society of which Her Royal Highness is Patron, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Surrey (Mrs. Timothy Goad).

The Duke of Gloucester this morning opened the Central Hub at the Federation of Jewish Services, Heathlands Drive, Prestwich, Manchester, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Greater Manchester (Mr. Warren Smith). His Royal Highness afterwards visited Willow Wood Hospice, Willow Wood Close, Mellor Road, Ashton-under-Lyne. The Duke of Gloucester this afternoon opened the renovated Altrincham Hospital, Railway Street, Altrincham.

The Princess Royal this morning visited a new manufacturing facility at Controlled Therapeutics (Scotland) Limited, 1 Redwood Place, East Kilbride, Glasgow, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Lanarkshire (Mr. Gilbert Cox). Her Royal Highness this afternoon visited the new Headquarters of L. S. Smellie and Sons Limited, Hamilton House, Station Road, Strathaven, Lanarkshire. The Princess Royal later visited the newly renovated Headquarters of Coalburn Silver Band, 27 Bellfield Road, Coalburn, Lanarkshire.

The Duchess of Gloucester, Honorary President, this evening attended the Lawn Tennis Association Reception in the Members Enclosure, the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club, Wimbledon, London SW19.

The Princess Royal, Patron, Royal Wanstead Children's Foundation, this afternoon launched a fundraising appeal on board HQS WELLINGTON, Temple Stairs, Victoria Embankment, London WC2. Her Royal Highness, Patron, Voluntary Service Overseas, this evening held a Dinner at St. James's Palace.
The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall this evening attended the Royal Variety Performance at the London Palladium, Argyll Street, London W1.

Princess Alexandra this afternoon visited the new Resource Centre for Resources for Autism, 858 Finchley Road, London NW11. Her Royal Highness afterwards visited St. Luke's Hospice, Kenton Road, Harrow, Middlesex. Princess Alexandra, Joint Honorary President, later visited the Cancer Research UK Shop, 34 College Road, Harrow. Her Royal Highness, Patron, afterwards visited the People's Dispensary for Sick Animals Shop, 30 College Road, Harrow.
The Duke of York this morning opened Box London, 64 North Row, London W1. His Royal Highness this afternoon received the Ambassador from the Republic of Turkey (His Excellency Mr. Ünal Çeviköz).

The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh this morning arrived at Kemble Station and were received by Major John Eyre (Vice Lord-Lieutenant of Gloucestershire). Her Majesty, Air Commodore in Chief, Royal Air Force Regiment, and His Royal Highness, Marshal, Royal Air Force, travelled to Royal Air Force Fairford and were received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Gloucestershire (Mr. Henry Elwes) and Air Chief Marshal Sir Glenn Torpy (Chief of the Air Staff). The Queen was received on parade with a Royal Salute and subsequently reviewed the parade. After the Colours were consecrated, Her Majesty presented the new Colours to the Royal Air Force and Royal Air Force Regiment. The Queen was graciously pleased to address the parade and the Chief of the Air Staff and the Commandant General Royal Air Force Regiment (Air Commodore Steven Abbott) replied. The new Colours were subsequently Trooped and the parade marched past Her Majesty. The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh were entertained to Luncheon this afternoon by the Chief of the Air Staff and the Commander in Chief Air Command (Air Chief Marshal Sir Clive Loader) and later viewed a flypast of Royal Air Force aircraft to mark the Ninetieth Anniversary of the Royal Air Force.

The Princess Margaret, Countess of Snowdon, Colonel-in-Chief, today visited The Light Dragoons at Haig Barracks, Bergen-Hohne, Germany. Mrs. Charles Vyvyan and Major the Lord Napier and Ettrick were in attendance.

Princess Alexandra this afternoon opened the newly renovated Urology Centre at Epsom Hospital, Dorking Road, Epsom, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Surrey (Dame Sarah Goad) Her Royal Highness later visited the Brigitte Trust at the Old Chapel, St. John's School, Epsom Road, Leatherhead, Surrey.

The Rt. Hon. Boris Johnson MP (Prime Minister and First Lord of the Treasury) had an audience of The Queen via telephone this evening. By command of Her Majesty, Mr. Alistair Harrison (Marshal of the Diplomatic Corps) called upon His Excellency Mr. Derick Ally at 130-132 Buckingham Palace Road, London SW1, this morning in order to bid farewell to His Excellency upon relinquishing his appointment as High Commissioner for the Republic of Seychelles in London.

The Duke and Duchess of Cambridge and Prince Henry of Wales this afternoon departed Royal Air Force Marham for France and were received this evening upon arrival at Albert-Picardie Airport, Méaulte, by Her Majesty's Ambassador to the French Republic (His Excellency Sir Julian King). Their Royal Highnesses afterwards attended a Vigil Service at the Thiepval Memorial, Rue de l'Ancre, Thiepval, to commemorate the Centenary of the Battle of the Somme. Mr. Miguel Head, Miss Rebecca Deacon, Mr. Edward Lane Fox and Lieutenant Commander James Benbow are in attendance.

The Duke of Cambridge, Honorary Air Commandant, Royal Air Force Coningsby, and The Duchess of Cambridge this afternoon held a Christmas Party at the Orangery, Kensington Gardens, London W8, for the children and young people of Royal Air Force Coningsby and Royal Air Force Marham.

The Earl of Wessex this morning departed Heathrow Airport, London, for Cayman Islands to mark the Quincentennial celebrations and was received on arrival this afternoon by the Governor (His Excellency Mr. Bruce Dinwiddy). His Royal Highness this evening unveiled the West Bay Wall of Distinction, George Town. Brigadier John Smedley and Mrs. Ailsa Anderson are in attendance.

The Queen arrived at Dyce Airport, Aberdeen, this afternoon and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of the City of Aberdeen ( Councillor Ms. Margaret Smith, the Lord Provost). Her Majesty subsequently drove to Balmoral Castle.

The Prince of Wales this morning departed from Royal Air Force Brize Norton for Jordan and was received upon arrival this evening at Amman Marka Airport by Her Majesty's Ambassador to the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan (His Excellency Mr. Peter Millett) and Prince Ghazi bin Muhammad (Chief Advisor on Religious and Cultural Affairs to The King of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan). The Prince of Wales and The Duchess of Cornwall were later received by The King and Queen of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan at Beit al-Urdun Palace. The Duchess of Cornwall, President, the Brooke, accompanied by Princess Dana Firas, this afternoon met veterinarians and junior park rangers at the Equine Veterinary Clinic, Petra National Trust, Petra. Mr. Simon Martin, Mrs. Amanda MacManus, Mr. Patrick Harverson, Miss Laura McCleary and Major Peter Flynn are in attendance.
The Prince of Wales, President, this evening gave a Dinner for The Prince's Trust.

The Duke of Kent this morning departed from Uganda for South Africa and later arrived in Johannesburg. His Royal Highness this afternoon attended a presentation Wheelchair Tennis South Africa event at Johannesburg University. The Duke of Kent, President, Commonwealth War Graves Commission, later visited the Commonwealth War Graves Cemetery in Johannesburg. His Royal Highness this evening attended a Business Reception given by the United Kingdom Trade and Investment in Johannesburg.
The Duke of Kent, Colonel, this morning visited the Scots Guards on exercise on Salisbury Plain, Wiltshire.

The Duke of Gloucester, Deputy Colonel-in-Chief, The Royal Logistic Corps, this morning attended the Conductors' Parchment Ceremony at The Princess Royal Barracks, Blackdown Barracks, Deepcut, Camberley, Surrey.
The Duke of York, Colonel-in-Chief, The Staffordshire Regiment (The Prince of Wales's), this morning visited the 1st Battalion at Mooltan Barracks, Tidworth, Hampshire.

The Princess Royal, Patron, National Association of Victims Support Schemes, this morning attended the launch of a new telephone helpline service at One Whitehall Place, Westminster, London SW1. Her Royal Highness, Patron, the National Autistic Society, this afternoon attended a Lunch at J. Walter Thompson, Hays Mews, London W1. The Princess Royal afterwards officially re-opened the Metropolitan Police Station in Savile Row, London W1.

The Queen and The Duke of Edinburgh left the Palace of Holyroodhouse this morning. Her Majesty drove to Dovecot Studios Tapestry Company, Donaldson College, West Coats, Edinburgh, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of the City of Edinburgh (Mrs. Martin Hinds, the Rt. Hon. the Lord Provost), the Chairman (Mr. Alastair Salvesen) and Mr. David Weir (Managing Director). The Queen afterwards visited the Scottish National Gallery of Modern Art and was received by the Director (Mr. Richard Calvocoressi) and subsequently attended a Reception at the Dean Gallery, Belford Road, Edinburgh. Mrs. Lilian Holdsworth (Deputy Lieutenant of West Sussex) was present at Gatwick Airport, London, this morning upon the Arrival of the Governor-General of Saint Christopher and Nevis and welcomed His Excellency on behalf of Her Majesty.

The Duke of Edinburgh, Patron, this afternoon opened the Max Nicholson Building and the Chris Mead Library at the British Trust for Ornithology, the Nunnery, Thetford, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Norfolk (Mr. Richard Jewson).
The Duke of Kent, President, All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club, this afternoon attended the Ladies' Finals at Wimbledon, London SW19.

The Princess Royal today visited Nottinghamshire and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant (Sir Andrew Buchanan, Bt.). Her Royal Highness this morning officially opened the new training centre at John Deere Limited, Harby Road, Langar, Nottingham. The Princess Royal afterwards re-opened the Winged Fellowship Trust's Skylarks Respite Care Centre, Holme Pierrepont, Nottingham. Her Royal Highness this afternoon visited the Galleries of Justice, Shire Hall, Nottingham. The Princess Royal, President, British Knitting and Clothing Export Council, later visited Nottinghamshire and Derbyshire Clothing and Textiles Association Limited (NADCAT), the Idlewells, Sutton-in-Ashfield. Her Royal Highness, President, British Knitting and Clothing Export Council, afterwards visited Faun Limited, Stuart Street, Sutton-in-Ashfield.

The Earl of Wessex this morning laid the foundation stone of the 450 Hall Performing Arts Centre to commemorate the Four Hundred and Fiftieth Anniversary of Hampton School, Hanworth Road, Hampton, Middlesex, and was received by Mr. Nigel Clark (Deputy Lieutenant of Surrey). The Countess of Wessex this morning visited Wheelrights and Coachbuilders, Number One Wheelers Yard, Colyton, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Devon (Mr. Eric Dancer). Her Royal Highness afterwards visited Pulman's Weekly News, Tindle House, South Street, Axminster, Devon, to mark their One Hundred and Fiftieth Anniversary. The Countess of Wessex this afternoon attended a Lunch to mark the One Hundred and Fiftieth Anniversary of Pulman's News at Forde Abbey, Chard, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Somerset (Lady Gass) and Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Dorset (Mrs. Anthony Pitt-Rivers).

The Duke of York, Lord High Commissioner to the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland, this afternoon arrived at the Palace of Holyroodhouse and was received by the Hereditary Keeper (the Duke of Hamilton and Brandon). Later Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of the City of Edinburgh (Councillor Mr. George Grubb, the Rt. Hon. the Lord Provost) surrendered to His Royal Highness the Keys of the City, which The Duke of York returned to him. His Royal Highness this evening gave a Dinner at the Palace of Holyroodhouse.
The Duke of York, Captain, the Royal and Ancient Golf Club of St. Andrews, today attended the Open Championship at Royal Troon Golf Club, Ayrshire.

The Duke of Kent, President of the Trustees, The Duke of Edinburgh's Commonwealth Study Conference (United Kingdom Fund), this morning accompanied participants of the Commonwealth Study Conferences Leaders Conference Study Tour on their visit to Microsoft, Cardinal Place, 80-100 Victoria Street, London SW1.

The Duke of Gloucester, Grand Prior, the Order of St. John, and The Duchess of Gloucester, Commandant-in-Chief, St. John Ambulance in Wales, today visited Cardiff and were received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of South Glamorgan (Captain Norman Lloyd-Edwards RNR). His Royal Highness, accompanied by Her Royal Highness, this morning opened the Headquarters of St. John in Wales at Priory House, Beignon Close, Ocean Way, Cardiff. The Duke of Gloucester afterwards visited the Millennium Stadium, Westgate Road, Cardiff. The Duchess of Gloucester remained at the Headquarters of St. John in Wales to visit the Youth Training Programme. Their Royal Highnesses this afternoon attended a Lunch given by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of South Glamorgan at Hafen Wen, 41 Llantrisant Road, Llandaff. The Duke and Duchess of Gloucester later attended the Installation of the Deputy Lord Prior (Captain Norman Lloyd-Edwards RNR) and the Prior for Wales (Mr. David Hugh Thomas) at Llandaff Cathedral. Their Royal Highnesses this evening attended a Gala Dinner in aid of the Grand Prior's Appeal for the Priory of South Africa at Holland House Hotel, Newport Road, Cardiff.

The Princess Royal, Colonel-in-Chief, The King's Royal Hussars, this morning received Colonel Giles Harrison upon relinquishing his appointment as Commanding Officer and Lieutenant Colonel Alexander Potts upon assuming the appointment. Her Royal Highness, Patron, Catch 22, this afternoon attended the launch of the newly formed Catch 22 network at Tate Modern, Bankside, London SE1. The Princess Royal, Patron, the Intensive Care Society, this evening attended a Reception at the London Clinic, 22 Devonshire Place, London W1.
The Earl and Countess of Wessex today arrived at Farnborough Airport, Hampshire, from Monaco. Mrs. Rosemary Hamer was in attendance.
The Duke of Gloucester and The Duchess of Gloucester, Patron, Friends of St. Paul's Cathedral, this evening attended the Advent Procession in St. Paul's Cathedral, London EC4.

The Queen today visited Combermere Barracks, Windsor, Berkshire, and was received by the Commanding Officer (Lieutenant Colonel Mark van der Lande). Her Majesty viewed the Household Cavalry Memorial, before meeting the families of Soldiers deployed in the Gulf.

The Duke of Gloucester today visited Greater Manchester and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant (Mr. Warren Smith). His Royal Highness, Patron, Construction Youth Trust, this morning opened the Information and Communication Technology Learning Centre at Falinge Park High School, Falinge Road, Rochdale. The Duke of Gloucester afterwards opened the Casey Group's new Head Office in Rydings Road, Rochdale. His Royal Highness this afternoon opened the Woodland Retreat at Fairfield General Hospital, Rochdale Old Road, Bury. The Duke of Gloucester later visited the Thinklab and Eco House at the University of Salford, the Crescent, Salford. His Royal Highness afterwards visited the Authentic Food Company, Sharston Green Business Park, Robeson Way, Manchester, to commemorate their Twenty Fifth Anniversary.
The Duke of Kent this morning arrived at Heathrow Airport, London, from Iraq. Captain William Maitland Makgill Crichton was in attendance.

The Queen, Patron, The Queen's Trust, this morning visited Lister Community School, St. Mary's Road, Plaistow, London E13, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Greater London (Mr. Kenneth Olisa), the Headteacher (Mr. Anthony Wilson) and the Mayor of the London Borough of Newham (Sir Robin Wales). The Queen listened to choir and orchestra performances, observed classroom demonstrations and met students, teaching staff, governors and charity representatives. The Duke of Edinburgh this morning visited the "Samuel Pepys: Plague, Fire, Revolution" Exhibition at the National Maritime Museum, Greenwich, London SE10, and was received by Dr. Pieter van der Merwe (Deputy Lieutenant of Greater London). His Royal Highness, Patron, Wildscreen, this afternoon held a Luncheon at Buckingham Palace. The Duke of Edinburgh, Honorary Member, Imperial Poona Yacht Club, this evening attended a Tiffin at the Royal Thames Yacht Club, 60 Knightsbridge, London SW1.
The Prince of Wales, Duke of Cornwall, this morning presided at a meeting of The Prince's Council.

The Duke of Kent, Colonel, Scots Guards, today received Lieutenant Colonel Rupert Kitching upon relinquishing his appointment as Commanding Officer and Lieutenant Colonel James Leask upon assuming the appointment.

The Princess Royal this morning opened Victoria Gallery and Museum at the University of Liverpool, Victoria Building, Brownlow Hill, Liverpool, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Merseyside (Dame Lorna Muirhead). Her Royal Highness afterwards opened the Constructive Skills Centre at Deeside College, Kelsterton Road, Connah's Quay, Deeside, Flintshire, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Clwyd (Mr. Trefor Jones). The Princess Royal, Patron, the Home Farm Trust, this afternoon visited a housing development at 12 Old Vicarage Road, Willaston, Neston, and was received by Her Majesty's Lord-Lieutenant of Cheshire (Mr. William Bromley Davenport). Her Royal Highness, Patron, the Home Farm Trust, afterwards visited a housing development at 5 Orchard Brow, Little Neston, Cheshire. The Princess Royal, Patron, the Home Farm Trust, later attended a Reception at the Macdonald Craxton Wood Hotel, Parkgate Road, Ledsham, Cheshire.

The Duke of Edinburgh, President Emeritus, World Wide Fund for Nature - International, this evening presented The Duke of Edinburgh Conservation Medal and held a Dinner at Buckingham Palace. By command of The Queen, Mr. Charles Gray (Marshal of the Diplomatic Corps) called upon His Excellency Dr. M. Sayeedur Rahman Khan at 28 Queen's Gate, London SW7, this afternoon in order to bid farewell to His Excellency upon relinquishing his appointment as High Commissioner for the People's Republic of Bangladesh in London.

Princess Alexandra, President, accompanied by the Rt. Hon. Sir Angus Ogilvy, this evening attended a Reception and Dinner in support of Sight Savers International at the Victoria and Albert Museum, South Kensington, London SW7.
